summary: sensivity ships a FiveM game-cheat with hidden Windows persistence, obfuscated loader, and hardware-fingerprint exfiltration
details: On require/launch, launcher.js writes an HKCU Run-key entry named 'OneDriveUpdate' impersonating Microsoft OneDrive's auto-updater. The value points to a bundled VBS (OneDrive.Standalone.Updater.vbs) that silently re-launches launcher.js via wscript at every user logon, with a detached supervisor process that continuously respawns a worker. launcher.js then eval()s a 135 KB obfuscator.io-flattened bundle (server.obf.js), which queries HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Cryptography\MachineGuid, hostname, and volume serial, SHA-256 hashes them into an 'hwid', and POSTs that fingerprint plus a license key to a hardcoded remote license endpoint on every check. The package also loads a 6.8 MB Windows native addon sens.node that contains FiveM aimbot/teleport strings ('Head','LeftHand','LeftFoot','Teleport','License Accepted'); launcher.js actively rewrites console output to replace 'sens.node' with 'program module' and silently drops 'program module loaded' messages to conceal its presence. Additional behavior includes writing.qrshow.ps1 /.yt.ps1, spawning hidden PowerShell windows with a spoofed 'Windows PowerShell' title, and enumerating chrome/msedge/opera/opera_gx/brave processes whose MainWindowTitle matches /YouTube/ to gate behavior on the victim's browsing. The combination of hidden boot persistence under a Microsoft-impersonating name, eval of an obfuscated bundle, machine-fingerprint exfiltration, an undeclared native cheat binary, and active log-scrubbing constitutes installer-side compromise.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
